text:
## Seeing a typical plain-text transcript

The plain transcript is derived, not stored. The format is dead simple — each turn rendered as `User: <text>` / `Assistant: <text>`, joined by blank lines (capture.rs:466-468). The numbered version (what EXTRACT sees) just prefixes `%4d| ` per physical line: [...] Then turns are assembled as `User: …\n\nAssistant: …` (`build_transcript_string`, transcript.rs:272). [...] ## 1. Content filtering — `extract_text` (transcript.rs:6)
Applied per message as turns are parsed:
- **Text blocks only.** From a content-block array, only `type:"text"` blocks are kept. `tool_use`, `tool_result`, and `thinking`/reasoning blocks are **dropped entirely**. → all tool I/O is invisible to capture. [...] - **`<`-prefixed strings dropped wholesale.** Any string content that (trimmed) starts with `<` is discarded — the harness-injected `<system-reminder>`, `<task-notification>`, raw tool output, etc. ("Human prose never starts with `<`.") [...] - **Role filter** (transcript.rs:72): only `user`/`assistant` survive; any other role skipped. [...] - **Empty-text drop** (transcript.rs:76): a turn whose extracted text is empty after trim is dropped. → an assistant turn that was *only* a tool call (no prose) vanishes completely.
